sections.paragraph:
ἰταμός, ή, όν (since Aeschyl., Fgm. 282; Aristoph.; Demosth. et al.; Lucian; Aelian; Ael. Aristid. 31, 5 K.=11 p. 128 D.; Jer 6:23; 27:42) bold, impetuous Hm 11:12.—DELG s.v. εἶμι.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Hm Hm = Mandates

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 485
